hub_subtitle: Inglês para Trabalho Remoto em Tecnologia & Quality Assurance

lesson.menu_title: Used To, Be Used To, Get Used To -- Talking About Change and Adaptation
lesson.menu_desc: Ana joins a fully remote team and talks with her new team lead about the transition -- what she used to do, what she is used to now, and what she is still getting used to -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 13 -- Change and Adaptation
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Claudia Veraldi | Lesson 13 -- Change and Adaptation
lesson.grammar_point: used to / be used to / get used to

listenings.text: Let's talk about change at work. When you start something new -- a new team, a new tool, a new city -- you leave your comfort zone, and that feels strange at first. The important thing to remember is this: nobody is used to a new routine on day one. You get used to it slowly, step by step. I used to think that adapting fast was a sign of talent. Now I think the opposite. The people who thrive are not the ones who never struggle; they are the ones who keep adjusting when things feel unfamiliar. So give yourself time. In the first weeks, everything is harder than it needs to be. But every day, the new becomes a little more normal. That is resilience: not the absence of difficulty, but the habit of adjusting until the unfamiliar becomes your new comfort zone.

Hi Ana, it's Camila from the design team, just a quick welcome note before our first call. I remember my own first month here, so I know how it feels. Honestly, I wasn't used to a fully remote team at all. I used to work in an office every day, with people around me. The first two weeks were the hardest. I couldn't get used to writing everything down instead of just turning to my neighbor. But it got easier, I promise. My advice: don't try to change everything at once. Pick one new habit a week. By the second month, you won't even notice the difference. If you ever feel lost, just message me, we have all been there. You're going to settle in faster than you think.

extra_audio.text: Here is how Ana settled into her new remote team. First, she accepted that the first weeks would feel unfamiliar. Then, she picked one new habit to adjust to each week. After that, she asked a teammate how they got used to the routine. Next, she practiced the new tools a little every day. Finally, after a month, she felt at home and started to thrive.
